title: TrialDesignBench
primary_task: Reproduce statistical trial designs from SAP or protocol and generate executable R artifacts
relation_to_trialcompiler: Strong reference for case packaging, traceable rubrics, reproducible code artifacts, trajectory recording, and safety gates; it does not yet evaluate cross-document fact consistency or amendment propagation.

title: InformBench and InformGen
primary_task: Generate informed consent forms from protocols with regulatory compliance, factuality, and inline provenance
relation_to_trialcompiler: Closest public benchmark for protocol-to-associated-document generation; narrower than TrialCompiler because it focuses on protocol-to-ICF transformation rather than a multi-document dependency and version graph.

title: ClinicalReTrial
primary_task: Iteratively redesign failed clinical trial protocols using diagnosis, safety validation, candidate evaluation, reward, and hierarchical memory
relation_to_trialcompiler: Useful loop and memory reference, but its reward is predicted trial success and its target is scientific protocol optimization, not controlled document compilation and consistency.

title: AutoTrial
primary_task: Generate eligibility criteria from trial descriptions with retrieval and hybrid prompting
relation_to_trialcompiler: Useful baseline for one protocol section; it does not manage whole-document facts, versions, or dependent files.

title: CTBench
primary_task: Predict baseline feature lists from trial metadata
relation_to_trialcompiler: Adjacent trial-design benchmark with list-based semantic metrics; its task is substantially narrower than document engineering.

title: From RAGs to riches
primary_task: Generate protocol sections and evaluate clinical logic, references, terminology, and relevance
relation_to_trialcompiler: Supports RAG and expert rubric baselines for authoring quality; it does not provide a dependency-aware change engine.

title: Clinical Trials Protocol Authoring using LLMs
primary_task: Generate clinical trial protocol sections from structured drug and study metadata
relation_to_trialcompiler: Evidence that structured metadata improves drafting; lacks the controlled fact approval and benchmark depth required by TrialCompiler.

title: AI-assisted protocol information extraction for improved accuracy and efficiency in clinical trial workflows
primary_task: Extract hundreds of structured fields from protocols with RAG and expert adjudication
relation_to_trialcompiler: Direct support for candidate fact extraction and adjudication at scale; does not by itself solve cross-document change propagation.

title: ClinicalTrials.gov Quality Control Review Criteria
primary_task: Check registration records and uploaded clinical study documents for consistency and submission quality
relation_to_trialcompiler: Authoritative source for deterministic quality checks, document metadata, version dates, and upload constraints.

title: ICH M11 Clinical electronic Structured Harmonised Protocol
primary_task: Standardize protocol content, terminology, and exchange fields
relation_to_trialcompiler: Strong foundation for the Trial Fact Sheet schema and machine-readable protocol representation.

title: TrialBench
primary_task: Predict trial outcomes and design attributes across eight tasks
relation_to_trialcompiler: Important context and baselines for trial prediction, but not a benchmark for document consistency or authoring control.

title: TrialReviewBench and TrialMind
primary_task: Search, screen, and extract evidence for systematic reviews of clinical trials
relation_to_trialcompiler: Demonstrates expert-grounded evaluation and human-AI collaboration, but addresses evidence synthesis rather than protocol document lifecycle management.
